channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Oby Kechere, Nollywood Actress and Director, Dies After Long Illness
item.description: Oby Kechere, a prominent Nollywood actress and film director, has sadly passed away after a prolonged illness. Kechere, who was famously known for her role as “Ms Koi Koi” in the 2002 comedy classic '...

item.title: PNP to deploy P800 million worth of body cameras for Unified 911 system
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) is set to receive body-worn cameras valued at P800 million this May to enhance its operations under the Unified 911 emergency response system. PNP Chief Gen. Jose ...

item.title: U.S. Supreme Court Strikes Down Louisiana’s Majority-Black Congressional District
item.description: On 29th April 2026, the U.S. Supreme Court declared Louisiana’s second majority-Black congressional district unconstitutional, ruling it as a racial gerrymander. The case arose after Louisiana redrew ...

item.title: Shift to Streaming Leads to Job Cuts in Kenya’s Pay TV Sector
item.description: Kenya’s television landscape is undergoing a major shift, as highlighted by the Kenya National Bureau of Statistics (KNBS) 2026 Economic Survey. Traditional pay-TV subscriptions have dropped significa...

item.title: Fatal crash at Portland health club leaves one dead, explosive device found
item.description: Early on Saturday morning in Portland, Oregon, a vehicle crashed into the Multnomah Athletic Club, resulting in the death of one person. Authorities reported that evidence of an explosive device was f...

item.title: Four injured in Brixton shooting, one in critical condition
item.description: A serious incident occurred in Brixton where four individuals sustained injuries following gunfire from a moving vehicle. Among those injured, one person is reported to be in a life-threatening condit...

item.title: Australia invests in community language schools to boost Asian language fluency
item.description: Australia is confronting a significant decline in fluency in Asian languages among students, a trend seen as a strategic challenge for the nation’s engagement with the Indo-Pacific region. The Albanes...

item.title: Rapid Melting of Thwaites Glacier Signals Growing Climate Risks
item.description: During the 2026 Antarctic summer, scientists observed significant melting at the Thwaites Glacier, also known as the 'doomsday glacier', which contains enough ice to raise global sea levels by up to 6...

item.title: Major Lotto Jackpots Remain Unclaimed on May 1, 2026
item.description: On Friday, May 1, 2026, there were no winners for the major lotto draws in the Philippines, the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO) confirmed. The Ultra Lotto 6/58 jackpot worth P75 million d...

item.title: COTU Prepares for Labour Day Celebrations in Vihiga, First Outside Nairobi
item.description: Preparations for the 2026 Labour Day celebrations in Kenya are complete, according to Francis Atwoli, the Secretary General of the Central Organization of Trade Unions (COTU). This year, the event wil...

item.title: UAE’s exit from OPEC and OPEC+: causes, tensions with Saudi Arabia, and global oil impact
item.description: The United Arab Emirates (UAE) formally exited OPEC and the broader OPEC+ grouping on May 1, marking a significant shift in global energy politics. OPEC, established in 1960, coordinates petroleum pol...

item.title: Crinums remain unbeaten while Fireballs recover in TNL Week 1
item.description: The Free State Crinums continued their flawless start to the Telkom Netball League (TNL) with a perfect record of six wins from six matches in Power Week 1, held in Durban. Their 56–36 win over the Ja...

item.title: PH urged to accelerate collaboration under Japan-led AZEC initiative
item.description: A Philippine think tank is urging the government to fast-track its collaboration with the Japan-led Asia Zero Emissions Community (AZEC). The call comes as regional cooperation becomes increasingly im...

item.title: Leke Abejide exits ADC due to internal crisis and legal disputes
item.description: Leke Abejide, wey dey represent Yagba East/Yagba West/Mopamuro Federal Constituency for Kogi State for House of Representatives, don announce say him don waka comot from African Democratic Congress (A...

item.title: The Challenges and Realities of Independent Book Publishing in India
item.description: Sahana Ahmed, the founder and CEO of Bare Bones Publishing, reflects on the emotional and financial challenges of being an indie publisher in India. She discusses the often misunderstood nature of the...

item.title: Nigerian Man Shares Why Kenya Feels More Livable Than South Africa
item.description: A Nigerian man, Olanrewaju, has shared why he would prefer living permanently in Kenya over South Africa, based on his personal experiences. He explains that Kenya has a lively street economy, making ...

item.title: Private funeral planned for OPP Sgt. Brandon Malcolm after fatal on-duty motorcycle crash
item.description: A funeral service has been scheduled for Ontario Provincial Police (OPP) Sergeant Brandon Malcolm, who died in a single-vehicle motorcycle crash while on duty on Highway 401 near Cobourg, Ontario. The...

item.title: Harriet Tubman and Philadelphia Abolitionists: Mapping the Underground Railroad
item.description: Philadelphia played a pivotal role in the Underground Railroad, serving as a major destination for enslaved people seeking freedom before the Civil War. After Pennsylvania enacted the Act for the Grad...

item.title: ADC Confirms Plans To Field Candidates For 2027 Elections
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) don clear any rumours say dem no go put candidates for the 2027 general elections. Bolaji Abdullahi wey be the party National Publicity Secretary yan this on Satu...

item.title: Ongoing Middle East Conflict: Israeli Strikes in Southern Lebanon and Updates on OFW Repatriation
item.description: As of May 3, 2026, tensions continue to rise in the Middle East following a series of military actions. Israeli strikes were reported in southern Lebanon, with smoke observed over the city of Nabatieh...
